description: Targeted dominance sweeps for the audit's unverified degenerate-play candidates, when their lanes next open. SOCIAL (fold into contest Stage 4): Recall/Corroborate/Prep stacking vs a global pool cap; boost-lookup vs Appraise value; coalition-vs-solo floor (ED-297 ratified coalition dominance — decide the solo floor). COMBAT (post-R3 re-hunt): armor-tier no-downside; imposition asymmetry; ability-stacking budget (ability_primitives.py enforces no cap while config prose claims one — fix the false claim either way). MASS BATTLE: split-vs-concentration evidence contamination already tracked ED-MB-0002; Company-scale thread-cost arbitrage rides ED-1010. All [OPEN — Jordan tuning]-sensitive: verify structure survives retuning before filing as defects. Full candidate list: designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/01_workings/{deferred_unverified,p3}.json. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction on PR #77; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/ed_options.md E-10 (audit §5)).

P0 docket / P1-severity FORK (no stated default -- Jordan's pick remains open per the ED-IN-0011 convention): reconcile the Debate->Domain-Echo keying conflict (audit finding N-4). scale_transitions_v30.md SS5.4 (PP-108) keys the echo by Persuasion-Track band and carries a loser-side Mandate penalty for institutional-authority holders; social_contest_v30.md SS6 keys it by genre won (Memory -> Mandate +1 in the precedent's domain; Projection -> +1D first Domain Action) with no loser row. The schemes agree only that Compromise fires nothing; neither doc references the other. Options: band-keyed, genre-keyed, or composed (e.g. band gates magnitude, genre selects the stat/channel). Blocks ED-SC-0007 (echo wiring) at the spec level. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-A; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction). · RESOLVED 2026-07-08 (Jordan ruling, AskUserQuestion): keying = COMPOSED — band gates MAGNITUDE (Persuasion-Track band → domain_echo degree: Total→Overwhelming ±2 / Decisive→Success ±1 / Committee→Partial=no echo, the agreed 'Compromise fires nothing'), genre selects the STAT/CHANNEL (Memory→L/Mandate canon-direct per SS6; Projection→I, the outward-initiative channel realizing SS6's '+1D first Domain Action' on the aggregate stat set). Reconciliation noted in scale_transitions_v30 SS5.4 + social_contest_v30 SS6. Implemented in sim/cross_scale/parliamentary_bridge.py (ED-SC-0006/0007). Unblocks ED-SC-0007.

P0 docket / P2 FORK (no stated default): resolve the 'Piety Track' name collision (audit finding N-4b). One name, two referents, three docs: the 0-10 debate tracker is 'Piety Track' in scale_transitions_v30/npc_behavior_v30/glossary (glossary.md:84 points its canonical home at conviction_track_v1.md) but 'Persuasion Track' throughout social_contest_v30.md; params/bg/ separately uses 'Piety Track (PT)' for an unrelated per-territory BG stat. Decide the winning name for the debate tracker, its single canonical home doc, and the rename/alias for the per-territory stat. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-B; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction).

P0 docket / P1-severity FORK (no stated default): choose the kernel's canonical Argue-pool formula (audit finding N-2). Two contradictory implementations are simultaneously live: the deprecated legacy stub implements canon verbatim ((Primary x 2) + History - Wounds + fatigue, floor 1; contest_legacy_stub.py:111-127) and is the formula the campaign loop would actually reach; the promoted kernel's Pool.size(faculty) = max(5, faculty*2+3) (primitives.py:208-211) is [SEED], drops History/Wounds/fatigue, and raises the floor from 1 to 5 (erasing the low-pool regime the 2026-05-28 diagnostic measured to 2D). Decision gates calibration (P4), the rolling-engine re-verdict (ED-IN-0013), and any Godot export. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-C; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction).

P0 docket / P2 FORK (cap value is Jordan's design number): cap the Recall/Corroborate/Prep/Findings bonus-die stack in prose BEFORE Stage 4 wires the channels (audit deepening of PR #77 hunt L-A, ledger KU-1). Doc math reaches +8D over base in exchange 1 while the genre/audience boost is already capped at +2D combined; the audit refined the hazard to doc-math-only -- all four channels are entirely unimplemented in the current kernel (zero code presence beyond comments/flavor), so this is a spec fix with no code urgency, but it must land before Stage-4 wiring makes it live. Decide the global cap value and whether Findings share it. Overlaps ED-SC-0001's social sweep item (Recall/Corroborate/Prep stacking vs a global pool cap) -- ED-SC-0001 verifies structure by sweep; this entry carries the prose-cap decision itself. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-D; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction). Decision packet prepared 2026-07-08 (pessimist-action-audit residual, ED-SC-0012): designs/audit/2026-07-08-pessimist-action-audit/decision_packets/DP-2_SC_KU1_stacking_cap.md lays out candidate numeric ceilings (A +2D / B +3D / C +4D / D one combined cap across both bonus classes) for Jordan to rule.

P1 consequence-spine work item (accepted): make the promoted contest kernel reachable from the campaign loop (audit finding N-1, UPHELD by independent critic). Scope: (1) route scene_dispatch contest resolution to the promoted kernel API (build_contest/resolve_contest), retiring the deprecated contest_legacy_stub path (the kernel currently has ZERO non-test callers; the only production call site, scene_dispatch.py:105-108, reaches the stub); (2) author the party-derivation bridge -- a canon-backed derivation of contest parties from aggregate faction state, preserving scene_dispatch's correct no-fabrication discipline (the module-level CONTEXT-DERIVATION BRIDGE GAP note, scene_dispatch.py:19-24, and the 'parties intentionally absent' deferral at :63,100-104 mean every contest scene currently defers); (3) extend the campaign regression (sim/tests/test_mc_v18_regression.py) to assert at least one resolved contest, closing the gap where the golden output is structurally incapable of noticing contest regressions. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-E; main report SS6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction). EXECUTED (2026-07-08): (1) scene_dispatch.py's contest branch now calls contest.build_contest/contest.resolve_contest, retiring the contest_legacy_stub.run_contest call this branch used to make (the package's own back-compat re-export for parliamentary_vote.py's PERSUASION_* import is untouched -- out of scope). The kernel resolves off the GLOBAL random module (resolver.py's own documented constraint, not this fix's to change) -- reseeded per-call from a value drawn from world.rng, not a fixed pin (massbattle.py:1826-1830's own precedent: a fixed random.seed() pin breaks batch reproducibility), then the prior global state is restored so nothing else observes the reseed; campaign determinism verified unchanged. (2) Party-derivation bridge: new scene_dispatch._emergency_council_parties derives both sides of the one live trigger's contest (Stability Crisis -> Emergency Council, scale_transitions_v30.md:137) from the SAME faction's own cited aggregate stats -- side_a=round(Faction.L) (institutional authority to persuade), side_b=round(7-Faction.Sta) (the crisis's own case for change), both floored at 1 -- an explicitly [SEED] design default, not itself a P0 fork, open to Jordan revision; proceeding=EMERGENCY_COUNCIL_PROCEEDING='guild_arbitration' (Panel = a seated bench, the closest structural match to a council among the 8 canonical proceedings; also [SEED]). No other trigger/actor shape is bridged -- combat and any non-emergency_council contest context still defer, unchanged. (3) sim/tests/test_mc_v18_regression.py gained test_mc_v18_resolves_at_least_one_contest (asserts >=1 resolved contest across the seed-0 golden batch). ED-SC-0007's echo-mapping is deliberately NOT wired here (still blocked at the spec level by ED-SC-0002) -- no `echo` context block is set from this branch, so echo_transport (ED-IN-0028) stays inert exactly as it landed. Consequence: contests now consume RNG draws that previously never fired, so the seed-0 mc_v18 regression golden (test_mc_v18_regression.py) legitimately moved (Crown/Church 50/50 -> Crown/Varfell 50/50, battles_mean 38.0->30.0) and was regenerated + repinned in this same commit, per that test's own docstring instruction; the F7 seed-42/n=8 golden (test_f7_smoke_oracle.py) and the echo_transport tests were verified UNCHANGED -- Stability Crisis happens not to fire in any of those 8 particular campaigns, so their named-zero assertions still hold (seed-dependent, not a bridge failure). Full sim/tests suite: 397 passed (was 396), 0 failed after golden repin. ED-SC-0011's live-dispatch acceptance criterion is satisfied by (1); ED-SC-0007 (echo wiring) remains open, now unblocked mechanically but still gated on the ED-SC-0002 keying fork. · PARALLEL-WORK RECONCILIATION 2026-07-08 (ED-SC-0013 Auto/Manual Duality): this ED was realized by TWO parallel implementations. (a) #96 (above) routed the PERSONAL-scale emergency-council contest to the promoted kernel (_emergency_council_parties) = the PLAY-OUT fidelity. (b) THIS branch added the FACTION-scale auto-resolve — sim/cross_scale/parliamentary_bridge.py runs a §10 Parliamentary vote each season (proposer=lowest-Sta / establishment=highest-Mandate / abstain), composes the winner Domain Echo (ED-SC-0007), through the substrate, flag-gated ECHO_TRANSPORT. Jordan flipped ECHO_TRANSPORT DEFAULT ON 2026-07-08 ("Yes echo transport on") — both #96's emergency council and this vote now run by default; seed-0 + F7 goldens regenerated for the combined behavior. Under ED-SC-0013 (a) is the play-out fidelity and (b) the auto-resolve fidelity of the same conflict class; ED-SC-0011 reframed as the zoom-in expansion calibrated to (b).

P1 consequence-spine work item (accepted; BLOCKED BY ED-SC-0002 at the spec level): wire contest outcomes into the world (audit finding N-5, UPHELD). sim/cross_scale/domain_echo.py is a complete, individually-correct implementation of scale_transitions SS5 with no caller anywhere; scene_dispatch.py:116 passes zoom_out({}, world) -- an empty dict -- instead of the contest result computed one line earlier (the self-flagged OUTCOME->ECHO MAPPING GAP, scene_dispatch.py:16-32). Scope: (1) map Bout outcome -> compute_domain_echo per the ED-SC-0002 keying decision; (2) call parliamentary_vote (the one module that already writes the SS10 Total Victory Mandate penalty to faction state) from the campaign loop -- grep confirms mc_v18 never reaches parliamentary_stay/transfer/vote. RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-F; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction). · RESOLVED 2026-07-08 (Jordan ruling; composed keying from ED-SC-0002): wired contest outcomes into the world. The §10 Total-Victory Mandate penalty is applied by run_parliamentary_vote (the parliamentary_vote-into-the-loop half); the WINNER Domain Echo is composed (band×genre) and emitted through the substrate (echo_transport → domain_echo → OF-7 deferred faction apply at the accounting boundary). keys_emitted 0→30 at seed 42; the KeyLog is now the campaign's first non-empty canonical_key_log. Also corrected echo_transport's apply units to STAT POINTS (× MULTS), mirroring the §10 adjust convention (was applying delta/MULTS while inert). Flag-gated (ECHO_TRANSPORT). No longer blocked (ED-SC-0002 ruled). · PLAY-OUT-FIDELITY ADDENDUM 2026-07-08 (two-session merge): this branch's sim/cross_scale/scene_dispatch.py ADDITIONALLY wires the Emergency Council PLAY-OUT echo -- its emergency_council branch sets ctx['echo'] on the Mandate/'L' channel, degree (Success/Failure/Partial) off the ballot verdict, firing through echo_transport under ECHO_TRANSPORT (seed=1, test_echo_transport.py). COMPLEMENTS, not duplicates, parliamentary_bridge.py's faction-scale auto-resolve -- played vs. auto-resolved of the same conflict class. Double-fire flagged (both can emit in one Stability-Crisis season): exactly the case under origin/main's ED-SC-0013 Auto/Manual Duality FORK-C (calibration tolerance, needs_jordan) -- not a new fork.

P2 hygiene work item (accepted): refresh references/module_contracts.yaml's social_contest entry (lines 425-447; audit finding N-3, UPHELD). The entry predates the entire Stage 1b-3 rebuild: resolver reads dice_pool where the kernel has been delta-sigma (sigma-leverage substrate, CR2) since Stage 1b; all five declared Key/state literals (state.opinion_revised, scene.contest_resolved, scene.dialogue, scene.insult, scene.threat) have zero hits anywhere in sim/personal/contest/; the entry lacks the godot_home and typed_params fields personal_combat's entry carries. A Godot developer binding to this contract would implement the wrong resolver against Keys the reference implementation never emits. Update resolver family, actual kernel surface, and add typed_params/godot_home (null-with-reason acceptable). RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-G; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction).

Stage-4 entry-criteria work item (accepted; fold into the ratified Stage 4 gate, not a new stage): (1) wire the Face/Rattled strain channel -- the CR3 three-tracker tradeoff triangle currently runs on two legs; Face is monotonic-up (primitives.py:83-90 states 'Standing.strip() is NEVER called... Face has NO strip/strain channel wired') and the strain>=Face -> Rattled -> -1D cascade (social_contest_v30.md:241-243) has no kernel consumer outside the single CR5 self-backfire trigger (audit deepening of PR #77 hunt L-E, ledger KU-3); (2) wire the SS9.3-9.4b thread junctures (practitioner weaving, between-exchange thread ops with the PP-351 temporal-axis conflict penalty, adjudicator thread response) -- the kernel has zero thread hooks, so the executable contest cannot express inseparability, failing P-14 at kernel depth (deepening of sim-kernel-no-thread-hooks, ledger KT-5). RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-H; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction).

P3 work item (accepted; cheap now, expensive later): give the contest kernel's narrative surface a perspective before any player-facing use (audit finding N-8, UPHELD). narrative.py's Chronicle/classify() carries no focalization/chronicler field and renders neutral omniscient summaries -- P-03 admits no neutral narrator, and prose doctrine routes chronicle-mode output through one of four named chroniclers. Also commission the Chronicle's first consumer: the structured per-bout record (winner, shape, turning point, lead changes, decisive appeal/ground) is currently consumed by nothing but its own test file, yet is precisely the artifact an arc-generator or convergence-detection feed would want -- the audit's cheapest genuine wiring win for the emergence feed (F-2 lineage). RATIFIED-AS-ACCEPTED by Jordan 2026-07-05 (post-merge instruction 'Ratify all' on PR #80; audit: designs/audit/2026-07-05-fable5-social-contest-audit/ed_options.md ED-SC-I; main report §6 sequencing P0 -> P1 (parallel P3-lite) -> P2 -> P4 adopted with the same instruction).

OPT-11, filed per Jordan's 2026-07-07 "ratify all" ruling (ED-IN-0026). Contest live-dispatch acceptance criterion: fold into ED-SC-0006/0007 -- the consequence spine is not done until scene_dispatch routes to the promoted kernel (today it reaches the deprecated raw-dice stub; the sigma-kernel has zero live callers). · CHARTER REFRAMED 2026-07-08 (ED-SC-0013, Auto/Manual Resolution Duality doctrine): ED-SC-0011 is the ZOOM-IN EXPANSION of the auto-resolver — route scene_dispatch to the promoted kernel by deriving named orators from aggregate faction state such that the PLAYED contest is distributionally consistent with the AUTO-resolved Parliamentary vote (parity harness = acceptance gate). Gives the derivation a target it previously lacked; see designs/architecture/auto_manual_resolution_duality_v1.md. · FORK-C RESIDUAL (ED-SC-0013, 2026-07-08): the auto/played calibration TOLERANCE (what counts as consistent, on which axis) is set here when the parity harness lands — the acceptance gate for this zoom-in expansion. Lean: unbiased mean is the hard anti-exploit constraint; variance looser for auto.

RATIFIED subtractive verdicts for the SC action set (ED-IN-0027; decision ratified, EXECUTION this SC-lane follow-up). Recall-citation + Pre-Contest-Prep REFINE — impose the missing combined ceiling on the +5D/+3D/+1D stack (KU-1; converges with the ED-SC-0004 pool-formula lineage, cross-corroborated by the 2026-07-04 refutation L-A); Appraise REFINE (narrow to the venue-fixed proceedings; resolve the fixed-at-setup vs senses-current contradiction); Wager Obligation DISTILL (institutional-collapse rows fold into the shared generational_transition Obligation-interruption rule; PC-death TRANSFER/RESET rows untouched). The 'select a deliberative game' meta-choice was critic-OVERTURNED to KEEP (four distinct mechanisms; the REFINE had judged pre-Stage-4 spec completeness — a cardinal-rule violation) — no action. Docket rows: ed_options.md SC section. · EXECUTED 2026-07-08 (SC-lane follow-up to ED-IN-0027, resolves this ED — design decisions made; numeric/kernel residuals flagged). social_contest_v30: (1) Appraise REFINE (§4 Step 1) -- audience/faction-boost read split off to free setup-screen knowledge (deterministic function of the public Faction Boosts table); the Appraise roll keeps only the adjudicator-armature judge-read, sharpened; audience-boost table retained until the setup surface is built. (2) Recall+Prep REFINE (§4 Step 3/§9.1) -- the KU-1 'whether to cap' question is settled: a single global stacking cap on the combined non-attribute Recall+Corroborate+Prep+Findings bonus is ADOPTED, mirroring the +2D genre/audience cap; the exact ceiling VALUE is deliberately left to Jordan's ED-SC-0005 ruling (P0, needs_jordan; distinct from ED-SC-0004's Argue-pool-formula fork), narrowing KU-1 from open to value-only. (3) Wager Obligation DISTILL (§6.1.1) -- four of five edge cases (counterparty death, institutional collapse, PC-death holding/owing) generalize to ALL Obligations via a shared interruption rule reusing generational_transition TRANSFER/RESET; only 'structural impossibility' stays Wager-specific; retained until §6.1 is refactored. params/contest.md carries a doc-side co-file note (no values changed). NO sim-kernel edits (sim/personal/contest/ is under active rebuild + ED-SC-0004 is Jordan's); the setup-screen surface, the §6.1 shared rule, and kernel wiring are follow-ons. Gates green. · UPDATE (same PR #104, 2026-07-08): the Wager DISTILL is now PHYSICALLY EXECUTED, not just annotated — a general 'Obligation interruption (all Obligations)' rule authored at social_contest_v30 §6.1 (counterparty death / institutional collapse / PC succession -> generational_transition TRANSFER/RESET, discharge-neutral if no successor within 4 seasons); §6.1.1 reframed as the Wager-specific overlay (arc-transition + Renown/Scar consequences) plus the one Wager-only case (structural impossibility). Plain Obligations now inherit the interruption machinery they previously lacked.

Parliamentary total-victory Mandate stacking, surfaced building the Censure fallback (ED-FA-0012, sim/provincial/parliamentary_action.py::propose_censure). sim/personal/parliamentary_vote.py's SS10 BG-Vote resolver self-applies a generic Total-Victory Mandate -1 to the losing coalition's dominant faction; faction_layer_v30 SS5.4's Censure tier separately applies its own target-effect Mandate -1 on a pass. Neither source states whether the two penalties compound on the SAME faction within ONE motion. Currently implemented as stacking (both fire, -2 total on a TV Censure pass) as the literal-faithful default -- NOT ratified canon. This generalizes beyond Censure: every future Parliamentary Sanction tier (Embargo/Blockade/Combined/Outlawry, per ED-FA-0006's parameterization) will hit the same seam. Jordan picks: stack to -2 on a TV pass, or cap the target at -1 total. Until ruled, sim/tests/test_parliamentary_action.py's total-victory-stacking test documents the CURRENT composition, not a ratified design intent.
source: sim/provincial/parliamentary_action.py:141-149 (ED-FA-0012 build session); sim/personal/parliamentary_vote.py SS10; designs/provincial/faction_layer_v30.md SS5.4

description: Auto/Manual Resolution Duality doctrine (PROPOSED) — drafted 2026-07-08 at Jordan's request (his Total-War auto/manual framing). designs/architecture/auto_manual_resolution_duality_v1.md: every contested event has two resolution FIDELITIES of the same conflict — AUTO-RESOLVE (algorithmic, aggregate-state, one step) and PLAY-IT-OUT (a full personal-scale scene) — and the zoom in/out protocol (scale_transitions §4) IS the toggle. The Parliamentary vote wired in ED-SC-0006/0007 (ECHO_TRANSPORT default ON) is the first auto-resolver; the promoted personal contest kernel is its played counterpart. KEY CONSTRAINT: the two must be distributionally consistent on matched inputs (E[auto]~=E[played]) — an EXPLOIT-PREVENTION requirement (no mode-shopping) whose acceptance oracle is a NEW parity harness between parliamentary_bridge and the personal kernel. Reframes ED-SC-0011 as the ZOOM-IN EXPANSION (a party-derivation calibrated to the auto-resolver, parity harness = acceptance gate) -- converting the previously-undefined derivation into a well-posed problem. Escalation policy: default-auto / opt-in-play, stakes-gated at scale_transitions 4.3.2. Open forks (needs_jordan, NOT ratified by merge): FORK-A one-engine-vs-two, FORK-B escalation predicate, FORK-C calibration tolerance, FORK-D player-layer choice-vs-forced. Per ED-1094 review+merge ratifies the DOCTRINE (duality + calibration constraint + ED-SC-0011 reframe); the forks stay open. Does NOT implement the harness or change the auto-resolver; the provisional derivation stays retunable (not canon). · REVISED + RULED 2026-07-08 (Jordan: "specific events on a slate" steer + "resolve A/B/D, keep C open"). Doctrine reworked to lead with the SCENE SLATE as the spine — player_agency_v30 §4 already specifies it (deterministic, priority-ranked, settlement-anchored, Conviction-biased set of SPECIFIC events; 3–5 scene-action budget; "opportunities not pursued resolve through NPC AI without player input" = the auto-resolve, already canon). Fidelity is a SPECTRUM (played / witnessed [player_agency Witness Mode] / auto), precedents FM/Total War/CK/XCOM/Disco Elysium. FORKS RULED: A one engine event-parameterized (RESOLVED); B escalation = the Slate's budget/priority/Conviction triage + Mandatory §4.3.2 (RESOLVED by existing canon); D choice-vs-forced = Mandatory/opt-in/Witness (RESOLVED by existing canon); C calibration tolerance the ONE residual (needs_jordan) — carried to ED-SC-0011's parity-harness acceptance gate. Chief build implication (separate item): event-parameterize the auto-resolver so it resolves SPECIFIC slate motions, not a generic per-season roll. Status flipped open→resolved.

Standing range collision (OPT-AV-12, attribute/value coherence audit ED-IN-0029): same-name 0-10 (BG faction track) vs 0-5 (contest kernel) range collision, plus a cross-scale homonym between the two (census C4-F9 sharpened). RATIFIED: the BG faction track's range is 0-10 per glossary (correct references/clock_registry_v30.md, which disagrees); rename or scope-tag one homonym half so 'Standing' unambiguously resolves per scale; add as a 4th entry to name_collision_database.yaml (note the existing :139 partial exemption predates this ruling). FA co-sign required (the BG-side carrier is faction-owned). Decision-ratified, execution deferred to SC (+FA co-sign).
